nodes.context: Colloquial term for various large-scale organized crime elements originating in Greece or operated by Greeks.
Organized crime groups from Albania operating in Greece.
Organized crime groups from Romania operating in Greece.
Organized crime groups from Serbia operating in Greece.
Organized crime groups from Russia collaborating with Greek criminal organizations.
Greek-American organized crime group in New York City.
Greek-American organized crime group in Philadelphia.
Ethnic Greek criminal organizations in Canada.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate based in Chicago, with Greek-American associates.
The five major Italian-American Mafia families in New York City.
Italian-American Mafia family based in Newark, New Jersey.
Italian organized crime syndicate based in Campania, compared to Greek organized crime.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate in the United States.
Organized crime groups of Armenian origin.
Organized crime groups of Kurdish origin.
Turkish organized crime family involved in drug trafficking.
Italian organized crime syndicate based in Apulia.

edges.evidence_quote: The domestic criminal syndicates additionally collaborate with Albanian , Romanian and Serbian mafia groups who operate in Greece.
edges.context: Greek mafia collaborates with Albanian mafia groups operating in Greece.

edges.evidence_quote: Arms, narcotics and illegal oil are smuggled by Greek criminal organizations, often in collaboration with Albanian or Russian mafia groups, from local seaports to important destination centers such as the docks of Naples or Antwerp.
edges.context: Greek criminal organizations collaborate with Russian mafia groups in smuggling operations.

edges.evidence_quote: For this reason, it is often compared to the Italian Camorra , which at times has operated via a similar model, although Greek organised crime relies mainly on smuggling whereas the Italian Camorra relies mainly on extortion and racketeering.
edges.context: Greek organized crime is compared to the Camorra, but no direct relationship is asserted.
